text.title: Gcp Arch Patterns Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example for the gcp-arch-patterns methodology; demonstrates the full procedure resolving to a valid output artefact.

example.content:
A retail platform standardises its production architecture using `regional-private-gke`, `cloud-sql-regional-pitr`, and `static-assets-on-cs-cdn`.
Inputs:
- Workload: e-commerce monolith (Django) + product-search service (Go) + static catalogue assets. - HA target: 99.9% monthly availability. - Compliance: EU data residency only.
Output spec:
```yaml pattern: gke_production components: - gke_regional_private_workload_identity - cloud_sql_postgres_regional_pitr - cloud_storage_static_assets_behind_global_lb_cdn rationale: | Regional GKE + Workload Identity replaces zonal GKE with node SA keys. Cloud SQL REGIONAL+PITR meets RPO=5min, RTO=15min HA target. Static assets offloaded to Cloud Storage + CDN; origin shielded. ha_strategy: - regional_gke_three_zones - cloud_sql_failover_replica_pitr_7d - global_lb_with_iap_fallback security_controls: - workload_identity_federation_for_ci - cloud_armor_rules_owasp_top10 - org_policy_iam_disable_sa_key_creation ```
Trace through the decision tree → root `pattern == gke_production` → conclusion ref=`regional-private-gke` → spec drafted → spec ratified → validator passes.
